name: The Principalities
summary: Imperial umbrella polity of noble territorial powers, imperial central authority, and a successor trade order centered on The Union, now carrying an explicit umbrella demographic canon.
identity: mixed legal and magical posture by territory
house-structured social identity stronger than a single unified civic demographic identity

governance_model.central_core: Emperor, imperial army, and imperial adjudication set umbrella-level sovereignty.
subordinate_layers: active noble houses controlling major territories
The Union as sanctioned guild-governed economic authority
regional institutions such as Roadwardens, ORE, Panim registries, and Magi Magistratus

bounded_population_signals: umbrella-level demographic canon now explicit in prior_release
narrower polity records override umbrella generalization where applicable
population_status.notes: prior_release formalizes the umbrella demographic base of the Principalities while preserving subordinate polity specificity.

cultural_posture: House duty, hierarchy, regional cultural distinction, public ritual, and negotiated coexistence across unequal powers define social life.
legal_posture: Law is uneven by territory: strong Roazian enforcement, broader imperial pressure, guild mediation, and localized legal cultures coexist.
magic_posture: Magic posture is mixed: broad social restriction, covert enforcement, selective elite use, and necromantic state use remain simultaneously true.
religious_posture: Divine appeasement, afterlife economy, contemplation, fear, and house-linked worship practices all shape public life.
economic_posture: Interdependent trade among house territories, guild mediation, and successor-market institutions drive the umbrella economy.
military_security_posture: Security posture mixes imperial force, Roadwardens, ORE, covert enforcement, house militaries, and guild pressure.
mobility_settlement_posture: A dense mesh of cities, roads, ports, floating cities, market hubs, and house territories defines mobility and settlement.
major_settlements_or_operating_nodes: house territories including Shelk, Roaz, Panim, Mimolot, Cosmouth, Zootia, Shirsh

known_absences: no full umbrella-polity race roster explicitly enumerated at nation level

unresolved_identity_gaps: Nation-level demographic enumeration remains incomplete; strongest population signals remain territorial, local, or institutional.
canon_note: Use polity-level demography files for narrow interpretation and the umbrella record only for empire-wide statements.
